task: Prevent qa-bdd from passing against a raw static PR server and then failing against the Cloudflare Pages runtime after merge.
decisions: Treat PR-versus-main target parity as the primary assurance defect rather than rerunning the failed main job.
Replace the PR Python static server with pinned Wrangler Pages local development so advanced-mode _worker.js routing is exercised before merge.
Do not use immutable Pages preview URLs because Cloudflare Access correctly redirects anonymous CI to its sign-in service.
Remove the chained workflow_run trigger so one main push produces one authoritative qa-bdd result.
Wait for every smoke route with a bounded retry budget before starting Cucumber.
Retry browser navigation once only for timeouts and network transport errors; deterministic HTTP responses remain authoritative.
Update the existing QA workflow contract instead of adding a parallel test suite.
validation: deterministic pre-fix PR/main target parity assertion: failed as expected
full Cucumber smoke suite through Wrangler Pages local runtime: 3 scenarios and 11 steps passed
full Cucumber smoke suite against researchops.pages.dev: 3 scenarios and 11 steps passed
GitHub Diamond standard workflow hardening validation: passed with no warnings
npm run lint: passed with the repository warning baseline and no errors
targeted Prettier, trace validation, trace coverage and git diff checks: passed
residualRisks: Cloudflare Access prevents anonymous PR CI from exercising the hosted branch preview URL directly.
Wrangler Pages local development approximates the deployed Pages runtime but does not prove external DNS, Cloudflare Access or edge availability.
The main workflow remains the deployed production smoke check and can still fail after the bounded readiness and navigation retry budgets are exhausted.
actionlint is not installed in the local workspace; the GitHub Diamond workflow hardening validator passed the edited workflow with no warnings.
